observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct (a) the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records so that errors and
adjustments could be identified and effected in the records, respectively; (1) and (b) the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents to
justify the validity of the book reconciling items and the journal entries made to record
the same. (2)

We recommended that the Head of Agency (HoA)/Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) and Municipal Accountant assure the submission of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) for the CA for confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents within seven (7) working days from the release of the check to the COA ATL concerned in a sealed envelope with a visible label “CONFIDENTIAL – For the COA ATL Only” and with a covering transmittal letter enumerating the submissions.

We recommended that the LCE require (a) the Inventory Committee to (i) facilitate a
complete inventory of PPE; and (4)
(ii) prepare a comprehensive RPCPPE wherein the report properties are grouped
according to PPE sub-major account with necessary details and thereafter, submit a copy to COA within the prescribed timeframe; (5)
(b) the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain complete property records; (6)
(ii) reconcile all records and inventory report periodically for the eventual cleansing of account; and (7)
(iii) establish ownership of the motor vehicles by providing the deed of donation or turn-over documents and if warranted, recognize it in the books, renew and update its LTO registration and facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable motor vehicle to avoid deterioration of its value; and (8)
(c) Municipal Accountant to (i) reclassify to Road Networks account the projects for construction of roads and road components as provided in the list; and (9)
(ii) to prepare CIPLC in accordance with Annex 12, Volume II of the Manual. (10)

We reiterated our recommendation that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to exhaust all means to gather the necessary data and documents for the unliquidated balances of the Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs accounts which were non-moving for eight or more years and if warranted, cause the liquidation, reconciliation, refund or write-off in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2016-005. (11)

We further recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to demand from the winning bidders a Performance Security in the form prescribed in Section 39 of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 with an amount not less than the required percentage of the total contract price; (12)
(b) Property Officer to explain the inconsistent dates in the DR and AIR; and (13)
(c) Municipal Accountant (i) to adjust the Due to LGUs and Trust Liabilities accounts by posting the proposed correcting entry; and (14)
(ii) submit the lacking documents, as discussed. (15)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management continue to maximize the use of DF for eligible projects as prescribed in the JMC for the optimal attainment of desirable socio-economic targets. (16)

We, however, reiterated our recommendation that the (a) Municipal Accountant check the completeness of supporting documents before effecting the payments of obligations in view of the contracts’ deliverables; and (17)
(b) BAC ensure that the Construction Safety and Health Program of the contractor is DOLE-approved. (18)

We further recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC to ascertain that (i) advance payments are made only upon submission of an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand; and (19)
(ii) the APP is prepared and submitted in accordance with GPPB issuance/s. (20)

We commended the Municipality for its compliance with Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121 as to the transfer of unspent DRRMF to STF. (21)

Nonetheless, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant present completely in the Notes to the Financial Statements the required disclosures pertaining to DRRM as prescribed in Item VII of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014. (22)

We further recommended that the Management direct the (a) MDRRMO and the BAC to follow the procedures when resorting to the alternative mode of procurement-Negotiated Procurement for Emergency Cases and prepare the relevant documents in accordance with the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; (23)
(b) MDRRMO and Municipal Engineer to (i) install visible warning signs to alert residents on the protruding steel bars and reposition, trim or cause their removal to prevent them from causing injuries; (24)
(ii) submit the plans/drawings and POW that contain the specific works to be accomplished on the rehabilitation project for evaluation; and (25)
(iii) ensure that rehabilitation of infrastructure projects charged to the LDRRMF are well planned and the safety of end-users are well taken into consideration; and (26)
(c) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the appropriate adjustment for the reclassification of the purchased Digital Electronic Siren with Early Warning System and Public Address recognized as Power Supply Systems into the DRRE account; (27)
(ii) post an adjustment of the CYs 2022 and 2023 equity account in order to correct the overstatement of expense pertaining to the transfer of unspent prior year’s LDRRMF; and (28)
(iii) submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team. (29)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE enjoin the GFPS to (i) pursue the implementation of well-planned targets in the GPB so that the gender issues indicated therein could be fully addressed; (30)
(ii) observe the budget limits of expenditures per planned activity; (31)
(iii) sex-disaggregate/quantify/specify the targets and results, as necessary; (32)
(iv) constitute the GFPS in keeping with Item 4.1.A.2 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and (33)
(v) submit the GPB within the period set in COA Circular No. 2014-001. (34)

We recommended that the LCE direct the MSWMB and MENRO Designate to (i) continue integrating pre-identified solid waste interventions in the SWMP into the Annual Budget and AIP; (35)
(ii) oversee the implementation and completion of programmed SWM projects and interventions to ensure that the intended outcomes are fully achieved; (36)
(iii) submit a comprehensive Accomplishment Report with information on the appropriations, funding source, expenditures, target/goals and accomplishments, in addition to the photo documentations and other reports already submitted to the Audit Team; and (37)
(iv) implement more effective waste segregation at source strategies, recycling and composting practices to meet waste reduction targets. (38)

We recommended that the LCE (a) enforce the authority of the Municipal Treasurer to examine the books of businesses to ascertain the correctness of assessment and collection of business tax; (39)
(b) facilitate the update and revision of the Citizens Charter in order to provide clear and specific set of documentary requirements for each type/line of business; and (40)
(c) instruct the BPLO to require applicants of business permits to submit the applicable documentary requirements and keep the documents on file for reference. (41)

We recommended that the Management continue withholding and remitting deductions in accordance with statutory regulations. (42)
However, we recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resource Management Officer (MHRMO) to (a) conduct reconciliation before remitting withheld taxes, contributions/premiums and loan amortizations to the BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC to avoid instances of over and under remittance; and (43)
(b) take the necessary measures in order to establish the accuracy of the balances of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts. (44)

1.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
collaborate to investigate and reconcile the noted differences in their records.
1.7 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the monthly
preparation of BRS for all bank accounts.

2.6 We recommended that the (a) Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal
Accountant, at the beginning of each year, a certified list based on the updated
RPTAR or Taxpayer’s Index Card, detailing all amounts due and collectible from
real property taxpayers; (b) Municipal Accountant establish the RPT and SET
Receivables based on the duly certified list from the Treasury Office; and (c)
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant coordinate to establish a complete
list of delinquent RPT and SET taxpayers, and ensure that the same is fully reconciled
with the RPT and SET Receivable balances reported at year-end.

3.9 We recommended that within seven (7) days from the release of the check, the
DVs with complete required supporting documents for CA for confidential
activities/projects be submitted by the Head of Agency (HoA)/Special Disbursing
Officer (SDO) and Municipal Accountant to the COA ATL concerned in a sealed
envelope with a visible label “CONFIDENTIAL – For the COA ATL Only” and with
a covering transmittal letter enumerating the submissions.
3.10 Moreover, we recommended that the HoA/SDO and Municipal Accountant
guarantee that all CF cash advances are fully substantiated by the appropriate
supporting documentation as prescribed in the JC.

4.9 We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant record the transactions
under Advances to SDO and Donations accounts instead of Petty Cash and Other
Maintenance and Operating Expenses accounts, respectively; and (b) Municipal
Treasurer ensure the (i) proper treatment of the financial assistance fund as cash
advances; and (ii) refund or liquidate unexpended balances by year-end.

5.18 We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive (LCE) create multiple
Inventory Committee to comply with the physical count of inventories, reporting it in
RPCI and submitting it not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; (b)
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer keep a record of their inventories
through SLCs and SCs for inventory management; and (c) Municipal Accountant
cleanse the dormant account observing the guidelines and procedures in its proper
disposition laid out in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

6.19 We reiterated our recommendation that the (a) Inventory Committee carry
out an inventory of PPE, report it in RPCPPE by type and thereafter, submit a copy
to COA within the specified period; (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer maintain complete property records for all PPE; (c) Municipal Treasurer
(i) register and renew the LTO registration of motor vehicles, as warranted; and (ii)
facilitate the disposal of unserviceable vehicles in accordance with the Revised
Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties; and (d) Municipal Accountant
keep a LC for each of the recorded CIP.
6.20 We further recommended that the LCE, Municipal Accountant, Municipal
Engineer, and Municipal Treasurer adhere with Local Roads Asset Management
System so that all vital data on roads and its road components for every road network
are reflected and recognized in pertinent reports and records.

7.10 We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant account for the employee
leave benefits such that a liability is recognized as they are earned; (b) Municipal
Budget Officer establish an Employee Benefits Reserve Fund to fund for all the leave
benefits owed to employees; and (c) Municipal Human Resource Management Officer
have a collaborative effort with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget
Officer for the strict adherence to Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing
Book V of E.O. No. 292 on the accounting and funding of leave benefits.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) record the 20% share
from the BFP Fire Code Fees from the TF to the GF, consistent with the intent and
provisions of DOF-DBM-DILG JC No. 2015-01 and ensure that the utilization of the
said fund undertake the regular appropriation process by obtaining approval from
the local Sanggunian; and (b) facilitate the reconciliation of accounts with the SAs
pertaining to said dormant balances and liquidate, if warranted, in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2016-005; otherwise follow the procedure for the proper
disposition of dormant accounts in COA Circular No. 2023-008 and request the write-
off of these accounts.

9.14 We recommended that the Management maintain the optimal utilization of
DF and its adherence to Section 3.2.5 of the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 for the
timely delivery of socio-economic benefits to local constituents while ensuring
compliance with regulations.
9.15 We further recommended that the (a) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
comply with the required posting of NOA and NTP within the required period in
order to ensure full transparency in the procurement process; and (b) Municipal
Engineer guarantee the preparation of the Statement of Time Elapsed to enhance
project monitoring and for easy evaluation of the timeline of the project.
9.16 We also recommended that, before entering into contract with the winning
bidder, the PE ascertain that the CSHP is approved by the DOLE, as an integral part
of the contract between the Municipality and the contractor.

10.17 We recommended that the (a) MDRRMO (i) maximize the utilization of the
LDRRMF by implementing all PPAs in the LDRRMFIP with the goal of enhancing
the disaster preparedness and resilience of the Municipality; (ii) reflect the correct
balance of the STF in the LDRRMFIP to effectively plan the allocations for PPAs to
be charged to the fund; and (iii) enforce the provision of warranty security in the
procurement of goods and supplies either in the form of retention money or a special
bank guarantee to avoid or minimize potential cost for repairs, replacements or
repurchases; and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) maintain a separate SL of the
unexpended LDRRMF for each year to accurately determine each year’s remaining
balance and the amount due for reversion to the GF at the end of five years, if any;
and (ii) record the necessary adjusting entries in the books to correct the noted
misstatements.

11.13 We recommended that the GFPS (a) expend the GAD budget within its limits;
(b) formally adjust and communicate all modifications to the originally endorsed
GPB and GAD AR, and subsequently provide COA with a copy of the revised GPB
and GAD AR along with the acknowledgment receipt from the concerned DILG
office; (c) properly plan and implement programs and activities under the GPB so
that the identified gender issues could be fully addressed; (d) sex-
disaggregate/quantify/specify the targets and outcomes, as necessary; and (e) apply
the HGDG tool for all the GAD-attributed programs and reflect them in the
“attributed programs” section of the enhanced GPB and GAD AR template.

12.9 We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor consider augmenting the
manpower complement of the Municipal Treasurer; (b) Municipal Treasurer
exercise its authority to yearly examine the books of accounts and other pertinent
records of businesses as this can produce additional revenue to fund essential PPAs
of the LGU; and (c) BPLO (i) ascertain that business permit applicants duly comply
with the submission of applicable documentary requirements and maintain the
documents in their file folders; and (ii) operationalize the acquired eLGU-iBPLS to
enhance the business permit licensing process.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource and Management Officer to (a) gather all pertinent documents and analyze the subject accounts to establish the accuracy of the undocumented balances of Due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth accounts; (b) remit all the due taxes and premiums immediately, when evidence warrants; and (c) reconcile the monthly withheld deductions with the amount of remittance to ensure that all deductions are fully remitted.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct – (a) the Treasury Office to update the RPTARs/ Taxpayers’ Index Card and subsequently, prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the  for LGUs; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of  and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.

We reiterated our recommendations: (a) that the Municipal Accountant (i) maintain complete PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof; and (ii) provide appropriate depreciation of all PPE; (b) that the Municipal Treasurer prepare PC of all PPE including properties acquired from previous years; and (c) to reorganize its Municipal Inventory Committee and direct it to conduct physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and submit to the Auditor concerned the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC and its Secretariat, the Accounting Office and the Engineering Office to exercise due diligence in preparing documents and processing disbursements and to ensure that required supporting documents are properly accomplished and attached to the disbursement vouchers. Furthermore, we recommended that the deficiencies noted in Annex A be submitted or settled immediately.

We recommended that the Management: (a) stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursement unless the item/service is petty in nature, which in that case, should be charge to Petty Cash Fund; (b) comply strictly with Section 93 of PD No. 1445 and the guidelines and principles of an imprest fund wherein regular, recurring and non-petty expenses should all be paid in check in the name of the supplier for internal control purposes; and (c) submit the lacking documents above mentioned  and support future disbursements with complete required supporting documents, to enable the proper evaluation of the transactions.  We recommended further that management comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 1051 in withholding the required taxes due to the Government.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed by the existing law (GAA provision) and avoid the issuance of certification allowing the acquisition of loans for employees and subsequently rendering their net pay to fall below the minimum. Further, we recommended that payroll deductions shall be limited following the order of preference provided in the GAA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) properly establish an MRF based on the guidelines set forth in Sections 32 and 33 of the RA No. 9003 and dispose the accumulated waste in a duly authorized landfill; (b) intensify further the conduct of Information and Education Campaigns to educate and make the residents aware of the existing environmental laws and policies with their corresponding fines and penalties; (c)  instruct the designated enforcers to impose the corresponding fines and penalties against violators to lessen the commission of prohibited acts such as littering, opening burning, and collection of non-segregated or unsorted waste; and (d) instruct the solid waste management board to continue to i) team up with the Sangguniang Bayan to be able to formulate or adopt more effective measures and policies in solid waste management, and ii) frequently monitor the effective implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal GFPS: (a) ensure that all the activities included in its GAD Plan are implemented to be able to attain their corresponding GAD Objectives; and (b) continue to comply with all GAD rules and regulations to promote gender mainstreaming.

We recommended that Management: (a) consider the timelines of project implementation and improve its monitoring functions or supervision for projects being implemented to avoid the incurrence of delays;  (b) direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the above-cited provisions of RA No.9184 and its IRR in the procurement activities and provide  justification on the deficiencies noted and immediately submit the lacking documents enumerated in the Table 2 above; and (c) ensure that the Construction Safety and Health Program is embedded in the project’s cost and ultimately in the contract or agreement between the LGU and the contractor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 and Advisory No. 2016-02 dated November 25, 2016 of the Interim National Peace and Order Council Secretariat of DILG relevant to appropriation, entitlement, release, use and reporting of the confidential funds.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Finance Committee (LFC) members revisit their mandate as provided by Article 412 of the RA No. 7160 Implementing Rules and Regulations, and heed the same. We also reiterated our recommendation that the LFC:
(a) Determine reasonably the projected income for the ensuing fiscal year;
(b) Recommend to the Local Chief Executive the level of annual expenditures and ceilings of spending for economic, social, and general services based on the approved local development plans; and
(c) Recommend to the Local Chief Executive the proper allocation of expenditures for each development activity between current operating expenditures and capital outlays.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balance with the accounting records to identify the recording errors committed and take the appropriate adjustments in her records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct – (a) the Treasury Office to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the list required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs at the beginning of the year; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to base the establishment of RPT and SET receivables on the list submitted by the Treasury Office for an accurate recognition of the said receivables.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure the proper grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by instructing the Municipal Accountant to a) strictly monitor the cash advances of officials/employees such that no cash advance is granted to those with pending unliquidated cash advances; b) make sure that said officials/employees settle their accounts within the time set by pertinent rules and regulations; and c) ensure that fully documented liquidation reports are submitted to the COA office.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to pay directly the suppliers through the issuance of checks. In cases where payment in cash cannot be avoided and is more practical and convenient, cash advances should be granted to LGU designated disbursing officers.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Inventory Team to conduct inventory taking of all the properties of the Municipality;  prepare and submit the result of the physical inventory that will be used by the designated Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant as basis for reconciling their records. Also, the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Services Officer to designate one of their personnel for the maintenance of PPE ledger cards and property cards per PPE, respectively. In case the concerned employees failed to render the required reports and maintain property records, enforce Section 122 of the P.D. No. 1445 which states that “failure on the part of officials concerned to submit required documents and reports shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements”. Moreover, we reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Team be reorganized and designate a Chairman who is not from the Accounting Office to have a check-and-balance in the inventory taking, since the inventory report will be reconciled with the records being maintained by the Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) instruct DepEd employees to turn-over to the Municipality the issued properties upon transfer to other school or town; and b) request the DepEd to inform the Municipality for every movement of DepEd personnel assigned in Bamban for the Municipal Treasurer to be able to monitor the proper turn-over of issued properties to the employees who will be moving out from a certain school.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC and its Secretariat and other concerned offices to submit the lacking documents for us to be able to make a decision in audit.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to transfer the remaining unexpended balance amounting to P6,184,217.05 from the General Fund to  Trust Fund in compliance with the above cited provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for proper monitoring of the utilization of the Local DRRM Fund. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide details of the Local DRRM Fund in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the aforementioned persons with unliquidated cash advances to settle immediately the said disallowance. Audit Disallowance not appealed within six (6) months from receipt hereof shall become final and executory as prescribed under Sections 48 and 51 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; and (b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that these projects could serve their purposes to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipality (a) organize the data from the CBMS and other sources to be able to formulate a GAD Database; (b) include in the GAD Plan and Budget the conduct of more capability-building and enhancement trainings, especially in the administration of the HGDG to be able to better identify and prioritize GAD projects and programs; (c) implement and accomplish all the activities specified in its GAD Plan and Budget to realize all the set objectives; and (d) continue its efforts in gender mainstreaming to be able to institutionalize GAD within the whole community for the benefit of not only a particular gender or sector, but for the benefit and equality of all.

We recommended that the Municipality (a) provide a budget to support the activities in connection with Solid Waste Management; (b) strictly implement its existing ordinances particularly Municipal Ordinance No. 02-97 and 01-2005 and make sure that every violator will be properly fined or apprehended; (c) expedite the process of finding a suitable MRF to contribute in the reduction of waste; (d) intensify further its Information and Education Campaigns (IECs) to educate and make the residents aware of existing policies regarding waste management for their own benefit; and (e) encourage every barangay officials to actively and continuously participate in implementing the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specially the proper segregation of waste at source, no littering policy, and no open burning of wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team. Continued disregard of the rules will constitute a ground for the disciplinary action against the responsible and accountable officer/s as may be warranted under the circumstances.

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to avoid suspension of the transactions and the penalty prescribed by law under 4.1 of this Circular.

We recommended that the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act No. 9184, or the Act providing for the Modernization, Standardization and Regulation of Procurement Activities of the Government be strictly adhered to by the Municipality.

We recommended that the members of the SB consider amending the ordinance on the regulation of the operations of three-wheeled public conveyance, specifically, incorporating a penalty for violation of not taking the outer lane of the national highways, to prevent undesirable outcomes which may pose hazards and imminent danger to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Revenue Regulation No. 2-28 dated April 7, 1998, as amended by Revenue Regulation Nos. 14-2002 and 10-2008 dated September 9, 2002 and July 8, 2008, respectively, in the withholding and remittance of taxes to avoid the misuse of the same.
